svg.aria-label: Three acts. One, megabrain_search: no-LLM retrieval ranks the chunks and the LLM rerank strikes the vocabulary-only matches. Two, megabrain_ask: a broad question fans out into three parallel sub-agents, one synthesis merges their cited answers with the verbatim code spliced in, and the workflow lands in the flow cache. Three, megabrain_graph: a path query between two files reports that they never call each other and names app.py as the file bridging them, each hop labelled with the function that carries it.

g.text: $ megabrain search . "how does the rerank drop noise"
19 signal chunks (31 pruned) · 2 ms · no LLM
rerank: one cheap LLM pass · ~1–2 s · fail-open
→ your agent reads signal, not a haystack
the rerank can only drop, never invent — retrieval stays deterministic
next ▸ what happens when the question is BROAD
$ megabrain ask . "how does indexing work end to end?"
classifier reads the bundle shape — BROAD → fan out · no LLM
Code splits over its syntax tree —
retrieval tools only · no file writes
Every chunk embeds in ONE batch
call — pplx by default, or any local
retrieval tools only · no file writes
Chunks + vectors land in one SQLite
file — incremental by sha, no vector
retrieval tools only · no file writes
synthesis — one model merges the agents' answers, citing their [[k]] spans
the model points · the engine splices
Indexing chunks code over its AST [[0]], embeds it in one batch [[3]], and stores vectors in SQLite [[5]] —
def index_repo(root: Path, force: bool = False) -> dict:
changed = [f for f in walk(root) if sha_changed(f)] # incremental
indexer.py L40 · verbatim, true line numbers
⚡ the whole workflow lands in the flow cache — a near-exact repeat then serves with zero LLM
next ▸ how two files actually relate
AST import/call edges · no LLM in the structure
└─ call/import → src/megabrain/app.py · via ask, graph, index, prune
✓ a real call chain — every hop is a resolved call site, verified against the AST
→ every hop names the function that carries it — a resolved call site, never a bare-name guess
so `re.search(...)` can never masquerade as your repo's own search() — and when two files DON'T connect, it says so
